sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left back pain
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing left back pain, which he describes as intermittent and often on the left side. He states the pain is not always present and can vary in intensity, ranging from mild to sharp. He notes that the pain sometimes moves and sometimes doesn't, and it's often described as a dull ache. He mentions that the pain is sometimes accompanied by muscle spasms. He reports that he has been drinking a lot more water and has been waking up more frequently to urinate. He denies any recent trauma or injury. He has no recollection of being tired. He has been eating normally and has no appetite changes. He is currently employed and is considering a new job. He reports that his previous boss was unsatisfactory. He is not currently experiencing any illness among his family members.
Findings
Physical exam reveals no tenderness to palpation in the lumbar spine. Range of motion is limited due to pain. Neurological exam is intact. No signs of inflammation or infection.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms and physical exam findings are most consistent with musculoskeletal back pain, potentially involving muscle strain or nerve irritation. Further investigation with imaging is warranted to rule out other potential causes.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am ordering a CT scan to evaluate the source of the back pain. I am also referring the patient to an ENT specialist to evaluate the recurrent strep infections and consider a tonsillectomy. I will order a lipid panel, a UA, a CMP, a CBC, and a TSH to assess overall health and rule out other potential contributing factors. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to review the results and discuss further management strategies. The patient should contact the office if they experience any worsening of their symptoms or if they have any questions.
